violations.description: AssertSidebarNavFromTypeCatalog re-arms by calling page.GotoAsync(startingUrl) after every sidebar target inside a 3-target loop. Each GotoAsync on a Blazor Server page triggers a full SignalR circuit reconnection + Type Catalog re-render. With 3 targets per test and 3 tests calling this helper (SidebarNav_From_Types, _TypesTabCommands, _TypesTabAggregates), this adds ~9s of nav overhead per test (~27s aggregate) on top of the actual assertion work. The final iteration's re-arm GoTo is also wasted work because the loop exits immediately after.
violations.suggestion: Two cheap wins: (a) skip the final re-arm GoTo when on the last iteration of the loop (saves one full nav per test); (b) consider using page.GoBackAsync() instead of GotoAsync(startingUrl) — browser back is typically faster than a fresh navigation because the previous TypeCatalog DOM may be served from BFCache. If neither is acceptable for the test contract, document explicitly that the re-arm cost is intentional (story rule says URL must transition back to /types, which is achieved either way).

violations.description: AdminUIE2EFixture is declared but never consumed by any test class or ICollectionFixture<>. Grepping the project shows zero references outside its own declaration. It allocates a WebApplicationFactory<Program> in InitializeAsync, but because no test references it, xUnit never instantiates it — no runtime cost today. However, the file is dead code, and if any future test wires it up as IClassFixture/ICollectionFixture, it will silently add a second ~1–3s host boot alongside PlaywrightFixture's Kestrel host, doubling fixture init.
violations.suggestion: Either delete AdminUIE2EFixture.cs (preferred — SmokeTests already uses IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>> directly with no abstraction needed) or wire it up to SmokeTests and remove the direct IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>> dependency to keep the design consistent. Choose one path; do not leave a dangling fixture.

violations.description: ShellRendersWithin2Seconds uses Stopwatch + ShouldBeLessThan(2000). Wall-clock perf assertions add no test execution cost but force CI runners to budget the assertion ceiling — if CI is loaded the test can flake near 2000ms. The same shape appears in BrowserSmokeTests.cs:87 (Dashboard_ShellRendersWithin3Seconds, 3000ms ceiling).
violations.suggestion: Wall-clock thresholds in functional tests are brittle and not the right tool for perf budgets. Move SLO assertions to a dedicated perf suite (e.g., k6, NBomber, or Playwright trace-based measurements) or relax the ceiling and accept it as a smoke-level guard only. If you keep the assertion, document that the threshold accommodates worst-case CI runners (the current 2s budget for HTTP shell is generous; the 3s budget for full Blazor hydration is tight).

violations.description: page.Console event handler is registered before each test and accumulates messages into a List<string> for the lifetime of the page. The overhead per message is small, but on a Blazor Server page with frequent SignalR diagnostics + Fluent UI hydration logs, the subscription handler fires many times per test. Same shape in Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests.cs:58.
violations.suggestion: Acceptable as-is — the overhead is negligible compared to the navigation/hydration work, and the assertion (errors.ShouldBeEmpty) genuinely depends on collecting console events. If profile data ever shows this on the hot path, filter at the subscription site (only Add when msg.Type == "error") so the list stays small — which the code already does. Leave as-is.

slow_tests_likely: Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests.SidebarNav_From_Types_TransitionsUrlAndVisiblePage (~12-15s — 3 sidebar targets x (click + URL wait + H1-detached wait + re-arm GoTo + H1-attached wait))
Dw5SidebarShortcutBrowserAtddTests.CtrlB_StorageKey_MatchesViewportTier_AndPersistsAcrossRefresh (~6-10s — full page.ReloadAsync() + re-hydration is necessary per test contract)

recommendations: Skip the final re-arm GoTo in Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests.AssertSidebarNavFromTypeCatalog when the loop is on its last iteration — trivial change, saves ~3s per call, ~9s aggregate across the three callers.
Delete the unused AdminUIE2EFixture.cs (no test references it). If kept, wire it into SmokeTests to remove the duplication with IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>.

Consider relaxing or removing the wall-clock Stopwatch.ShouldBeLessThan(2000/3000) assertions in SmokeTests.ShellRendersWithin2Seconds and BrowserSmokeTests.Dashboard_ShellRendersWithin3Seconds — these are functional smoke guards, not SLO tests, and CI flakiness near the ceiling is a known anti-pattern.

Leave the rest as-is: PlaywrightFixture's single-browser + single-Kestrel-host design is exemplary, every wait is condition-based (no Task.Delay / WaitForTimeoutAsync anywhere in the suite), and no test boots DAPR/Aspire infrastructure unnecessarily.

Future-proofing: if test count in the Playwright collection grows beyond ~30, consider enabling parallel test execution within the collection by giving each test its own context+page (already done) and switching to xUnit v3's CollectionBehaviorAttribute(MaxParallelThreads=N) — the fixture is already thread-safe (CreatePageAsync allocates fresh contexts) so this is mostly a config change.
summary: Fixture design is fundamentally sound (shared browser+host, condition-based waits throughout, no hard sleeps, no unnecessary infrastructure boot); the only meaningful performance cost is the redundant re-arm GoToAsync inside the Dw5TypeCatalogNavigation loop (~9s aggregate overhead), with three minor LOW issues (dead AdminUIE2EFixture class, two wall-clock perf assertions).
